notes: Opening turn work notes. Objective: plaintiff opening for proposition that by May 24, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, Iran initiated a major non-weather closure of its airspace under market rule. Record checked via get_case and list_evidence; only five initial case-packet files, no party submissions. Read exact contents of all five packet files. Packet findings: market-page ev_6c996... preserves rule text and operative elements; official-source-record ev_72c... says no merits official-source packet has been built and directs search for Iranian aviation authority records/NOTAMs/airport records/credible reporting; primary-evidence ev_9f806... says no pre-built merits packet and prioritizes CAO/CAA announcements, NOTAMs, airport-level suspension records, credible reporting, and cautious flight tracking; secondary-reporting ev_4a5... says no reporting packet yet; unresolved-gaps ev_f994... lists gaps including UMA rationale, triggering report, official records, Iran-initiated versus airline/foreign action, broad enough versus limited, and exact ET timing. Source map: strongest primary sources are Iranian Civil Aviation Organization/Authority, Iran Airports and Air Navigation Company, actual NOTAM reportedly OIIX A1010/26, and airport records for IKA/THR/MHD/SYZ/IFN. Confirming sources: Reuters/AP/IRNA/Mehr/Gulf News/Indian Express/NewsX/SafeAirspace/OPSGROUP; flight tracking only cautiously. Adverse sources: claims of no new NOTAM/fake news; market timeline language that closure was western-only, temporary, daylight-only, ended May 25, and maybe not a full major closure. Native searches performed: broad query for Iran closes airspace May 24 2026; domain query site:caa.gov.ir; NOTAM query; Polymarket query. Leads found, not record evidence yet: Indian Express says western half airspace temporarily closed May 22-May 25 and CAA issued NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 ordering shutdown of all airports in western Tehran FIR; Polymarket timeline includes similar leads but also adverse/no-broader-shutdown language. No submit_evidence allowed in opening, so opening will not rely on outside leads as record proof. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with search path, source targets, adverse checks, and unresolved gaps. Draft theory: admitted packet proves rule/gaps; likely merits case will turn on whether official NOTAM and airport records show an Iran-initiated, non-weather, major regional closure before cutoff; major Iranian airspace region language is key. Missing links: actual NOTAM, official Iranian source, exact affected airports/timing, and adverse fake-news/no-new-NOTAM verification.

Turn: openings:defendant. Objective: defendant opening. Record inspected with get_case/list_evidence and direct read_evidence_range for all five packet files. Current record has no merits evidence/exhibits; only market rule/API/page capture, official-source note, primary-evidence note, secondary-reporting note, unresolved-gaps note, and plaintiff opening. Evidence analysis: market-page packet proves governing elements and examples only; it does not prove a May 24 event. Official-source and primary-evidence packets affirm no merits source package exists and identify source categories needed: Iranian aviation authority/CAO/CAA records, NOTAMs, airport records, cautious flight tracking, credible reporting. Unresolved-gaps packet affirmatively leaves missing: triggering event/report, official source chain, exact ET timing, affected region/airports, whether observed cancellations were Iran-initiated, and whether the restriction was broad rather than limited/foreign/airline/weather/military-drill.

Pre-opening investigation/source map: searched web for Iran closes airspace May 24 2026 NOTAM commercial flights; site:cao.ir Iran airspace May 24 2026 closure NOTAM; Iran airspace closed May 24 2026 Reuters; Iran issues airspace notice military drills Strait of Hormuz May 2026. Opened Indian Express May 23 page and SafeAirspace Iran page; X did not render useful text. Preliminary unadmitted leads suggest a possible May 22-May 25 western Tehran FIR NOTAM (reported as OIIX A1010/26) and later partial reopening/western closure references; also many foreign warnings/operator avoidance materials that would not independently satisfy the rule. These are not admitted evidence and cannot be cited as proof in opening, but they shape the source map for later evidence phase.

Defense theory for opening: Current record proves the rule and the gaps, not the proposition. Plaintiff must prove by preponderance a traceable Iranian act in force by May 24 11:59 PM ET, non-weather cause, and qualifying scale/commercial effect. A major regional closure could qualify if proven, but the defense will test official attribution, source custody/provenance, ET validity period, whether western FIR is a major Iranian region, whether at least two listed airports were completely suspended, and whether the facts show broad commercial closure rather than limited approvals, daylight-only restrictions, airline avoidance, foreign warnings, weather, or narrow military-drill restrictions. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with source targets and unresolved gaps.

Merits turn notes. Opportunity arguments:plaintiff ready; limits: text 6000 chars, exhibits 9, submitted evidence 8, technical reports 3. Checked get_case/list_evidence: record contains plaintiff and defendant openings, five initial case-packet items only, no party evidence before my submissions. Defendant opening argues no raw NOTAM/official record and highlights initiation/timing/scope/provenance gaps. Built source map from opening journal. Searched exact NOTAM identifier and phrases: "OIIX A1010/26"; "western Tehran FIR" "May 25" Iran NOTAM; "all airports in the western" "Tehran FIR"; "Iran" "OIIX A1010". Opened and captured sources: Indian Express article May 23 2026 saying Iran closed western half May 22-May 25, CAA issued NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 shutting all airports in western Tehran FIR, eight airports remained operational including THR/IKA/IFN/SYZ/MHD, daylight-only and fresh CAA approval; submitted as ev_6fb47d9ec1a6_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-6fb47d9ec1a6. AbdiMedia article timestamp 2026/05/23 13:40 translating/recounting NOTAM A1010/26: all airports western Tehran FIR closed except limited listed airports, all prior permissions canceled, new CAO authorization required for IFR passenger flights, valid May 22 00:16 Iran time to May 25 12:00 Iran time; submitted as ev_1cffd8dd281e_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-1cffd8dd281e. Defcon Alerts public preview dated May 22: title/summary says NOTAM OIIX A1010/26, all airports in western Tehran FIR closed, validity May 22 05:46 UTC to May 25 08:30 UTC; paid body not captured; submitted as ev_63e27faf0dd2_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-63e27faf0dd2. Also opened Siasat article reporting NOTAM A1010/26 and daylight approvals; not submitted because cumulative and limited evidence budget/time. Adverse checks searched "no new NOTAM" and fake-news terms; accessible results were mainly social snippets/Roya/Instagram/Manifold claims that later reports said no new NOTAM or fake news; did not capture primary Mehr/CAO denial in time, unresolved for rebuttal. Raw official NOTAM/CAA record still not captured, so argument must state custody weakness. Reasoning: three admitted captures converge on identifier, Iranian authority, western Tehran FIR/all airports, May 22-May 25 validity, daylight exceptions/per-flight approval. Rule allows major Iranian airspace region and limited exceptions do not disqualify. Timing: May 25 08:30 UTC / noon Iran is after May 24 11:59 PM ET (May 25 03:59 UTC). Non-weather: sources tie to security/military tensions; no weather reason. Scope: best prong is major-region closure, not necessarily separate two-listed-airport complete suspension because several listed airports remained as limited-operation exceptions. Filing will offer three submitted evidence IDs plus market-page and packet gap files as needed and include a technical report/search ledger.

Turn: arguments:defendant. Record inspection done via get_case/list_evidence and direct reads of plaintiff's three submitted evidence items. Plaintiff evidence: ev_6fb47 Indian Express, ev_1cff AbdiMedia, ev_63e Defcon Alerts; all are text captures/extractions, not raw official NOTAM, and each includes custody limits acknowledging secondary source or paywall/aggregation. Plaintiff argument relies on convergence that A1010/26 was Iran-issued and covered western Tehran FIR airports through cutoff.

Investigation: searched exact NOTAM identifiers and adverse phrases: "OIIX A1010/26"; "A1010/26" "Tehran FIR"; "no new NOTAM" "western flight suspension remains in place" Iran May 2026; "Iran says no new NOTAM issued" "western flight suspension". Found Notamify page https://notamify.com/notams/OIIX/693f2d0f-54d3-433b-8128-49f5475ba269 with raw NOTAM text. Submitted it as defendant evidence ev_84fb9b1410dc_submitted-evidence-04-defendant-84fb9b1410dc. Notamify details: A1010/26, EST, category AD Airport Operations, UTC 2026-05-22 20:46 to 2026-05-25 08:30, raw Q line Q)OIIX/QFALC/IV/NBO/A/000/999/, E text closes all airports in west part of Tehran FIR except OITR, OIKK, OIAA, OISS, OIYY, OICC, OIGG, OIAW sunrise-sunset; previous permissions suspended; new CAA permission needed for civil IFR passenger flights on these airports.

Analysis: Notamify is adverse in confirming NOTAM likely existed and extended past cutoff, but useful for narrowing scope. Best defense: record proves at most an airport-operations NOTAM affecting airports in western Tehran FIR, not a broad closure of Iranian airspace or transiting commercial flights; it left eight airports operational sunrise-sunset with new CAA permission, including at least Shiraz among listed market airports. Plaintiff's airport-prong not proven because no airport ops data shows complete suspension of two listed airports. Plaintiff's exhibits conflict with raw/captured text on timing and exception airports: Defcon says May 22 05:46 UTC but Notamify B field says 20:46 UTC; Indian Express lists IKA/THR/IFN/SYZ/MHD among operational airports while AbdiMedia/Notamify list OITR/OIKK/OIAA/OISS/OIYY/OICC/OIGG/OIAW. No authenticated Iranian CAA/CAO source or NOTAM archive admitted. Social snippets about later "no new NOTAM"/Mehr denial were not captured as primary evidence and should not be cited as proof. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Rebuttal turn notes. Opportunity rebuttals:plaintiff ready; text limit 4000 chars, remaining exhibits 8, submitted evidence 5. Checked list_evidence and read defendant evidence exact contents. New defense evidence: ev_84fb9b1410dc_submitted-evidence-04-defendant-84fb9b1410dc, Notamify raw text for OIIX A1010/26. Exact capture says A1010/26 NOTAMR A0799/26; Q)OIIX/QFALC/IV/NBO/A/000/999; A)OIIX B)2605222046 C)2605250830EST; E) REF NOTAM A0796/26, all airports in west part of Tehran FIR are closed except OITR, OIKK, OIAA, OISS, OIYY, OICC, OIGG, OIAW operational from sunrise to sunset; all previous permissions suspended for all operators; new permission requested from CAA for operation of civil IFR passenger flights on these airports. Notamify category AD Airport Operations; page status inactive; custody limit aggregator not official Iranian archive. Defense argues this proves only airport operations, not airspace/transit closure, and exceptions are substantial. Plaintiff rebuttal theory: defendant evidence confirms Iranian authority/CAA involvement, timing through cutoff, region (west part of Tehran FIR), default closure of all airports in that region, all previous permissions suspended for all operators, new CAA permission required for civil IFR passenger flights. Market rule allows a major Iranian airspace region and limited exceptions; it does not require nationwide closure or explicit overflight-route closure when the operative suspension is of commercial arrivals/departures/airport operations throughout a major region. Airport operation classification is not a safe harbor; commercial flights arrive/depart through airports. Use DX-1 as plaintiff rebuttal exhibit; no new evidence needed. Journal updated.

Turn: surrebuttals:defendant. Inspected current record via get_case/list_evidence and re-read DX-1 Notamify evidence. Plaintiff rebuttal adds no new external source; it re-offers defendant Notamify evidence as PX-R1/DX-1 and argues that the rule is functional, not dependent on NOTAM taxonomy. Strongest rebuttal point: DX-1 raw text confirms a default airport closure and prior-permission suspension, with new CAA permission required, so plaintiff says this is functionally a broad commercial-flight suspension in west Tehran FIR and the limited exceptions clause saves it.

Surrebuttal analysis: No additional outside source needed because rebuttal is interpretive and turns on existing market rule plus DX-1 exact wording. Defense should concede source/timing where appropriate but insist the functional effect shown by raw text remains narrower than the rule: QFALC/AD airport operations; E text only closes airports in west part of Tehran FIR, not FIR/airspace/routes/overflights/ATS; eight airports remain operational sunrise-sunset with new permission; rule requires broad closure/suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from Iranian airspace or major region and excludes limited/partial closures. Limited exceptions clause applies only after broad closure is established; it cannot convert a partial airport-permission regime into a qualifying major airspace closure. Plaintiff also still lacks listed-airport prong proof and official Iranian archive/source chain. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Closing turn notes. Opportunity closings:plaintiff ready; text limit 5000; no evidence submission allowed. Checked get_case and list_evidence. Final record includes plaintiff merits/rebuttal, defendant argument/surrebuttal, plaintiff submitted evidence PX-1/PX-2/PX-3, defendant Notamify DX-1, and case packet. No new evidence after DX-1. Defendant surrebuttal argues function not taxonomy but says DX-1 proves only airport-permission restrictions, not transit/overflight closure; concedes likely non-weather NOTAM restricting airport operations in western Tehran FIR through cutoff. Closing theory: the decisive evidence is DX-1 raw text corroborated by PX-1/PX-2/PX-3. DX-1 text: all airports in west part of Tehran FIR are closed except listed airports operational sunrise to sunset; all previous permissions suspended for all operators; new CAA permission required for civil IFR passenger flights; applicability to May 25 08:30 UTC. This proves Iranian CAA/aviation authority initiation, timing after May 24 23:59 ET, non-weather/security context, and broad commercial flight suspension in a major Iranian region. Strongest contrary point is airport operations vs transit/overflight language; response: rule covers broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights in a major Iranian airspace region, limited exceptions do not disqualify, and arrivals/departures through airports are core commercial flights in the region. Weakness to concede: no official Iranian archive/state-hosted NOTAM; Notamify aggregator. Weight answer: preponderance plus defense's own text-specific evidence and consistent corroborating captures. Journal updated.

Turn: closings:defendant. Inspected current record via get_case/list_evidence and re-read DX-1. Plaintiff closing adds no new evidence and argues DX-1 plus PX-1/PX-3 establish all elements by preponderance; plaintiff accepts no state-hosted Iranian archive but says exact text and corroboration suffice. Strongest plaintiff closing point: the rule does not require nationwide/exceptionless shutdown and limited exceptions are permitted.

Closing analysis: Defense should not overcontest existence/timing/non-weather. Record likely proves an Iranian NOTAM through cutoff. The decisive closing point is fit with 'major closure' rule. DX-1 exact text is AD Airport Operations/QFALC, closes all airports in west part of Tehran FIR except eight, sunrise-sunset, with new permission for civil IFR passenger flights on those airports. It does not close FIR, airspace, ATS routes, overflights, or all commercial transit. Market rule requires broad closure/suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from airspace/major region and says limited/partial closures do not qualify. Limited-exceptions clause should not be used to transform a partial airport-permission regime into a major closure before breadth is proven. Plaintiff lacks two-listed-airport prong proof and official airport operations records. Source chain remains incomplete, but even crediting DX-1 the proven fact is narrower than the proposition. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.
